    Global Search Dialog
      1. Our Vision
      2. Focus Areas
      3. Equitable Community Development

      Equitable Community Development

      Transforming neighborhoods to improve health and wellbeing.

      Community development organizations and leaders help create neighborhoods where residents have what they need to thrive where they live, learn, work, and play.

      RWJF believes every community in every ZIP code should be a place where the people there can live their healthiest lives. While there is no single vision for what a healthy community looks like, community development—the work of creating the conditions so all residents can thrive—is key to reaching this vision. 

      Community Development Evolution

       

       

      What happens when the health and community development sectors realize they’re working toward the same goal of enabling people to live their healthiest lives?

      Watch this video to learn how the field of community development evolved in the past decade to help communities meet residents’ needs and address the effects of racism embedded in policies and practices.

      The Future of Community Development

      What are opportunities for the future of community development? We asked six experts.

      Featured Initiatives

      RWJF funds many initiatives to create opportunities that improve health equity. For this area of focus, these initiatives were selected to demonstrate grantmaking that is helping us achieve the greatest impact.

      Purpose Built Communities

      Learn more

      Center for Community Investment

      Learn more

      Healthy Neighborhoods Study

      Learn more

      Build Healthy Places Network

      Learn more

      Third Space Action Lab

      Learn more

      Invest Health

      Learn more

      Partners for Rural Transformation

      Learn more

      Impact Investments for Community Development

      The community development finance system (infographic)—made up of mission-driven lenders (infographic)—is a main driver of capital for essentials like affordable housing, clean water, and small businesses that create jobs and generational wealth in communities that historically have experienced a lack of investment, especially communities of color and those with low incomes. Our impact investments and related grants focus on strengthening the community development finance system.

      Increasing Economic Opportunities—Supporting Financial Institutions

      To boost financial opportunity in communities with economic and racial segregation, RWJF dedicated a $10 million investment to the Opportunity Finance Network for its Finance Justice Fund.

      Investing in Latino Communities

      A $4 million investment from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation to the National Association for Latino Community Asset Builders (NALCAB) will increase access to capital for Latino communities.

      Advancing Financial Inclusion by Strengthening Credit Unions

      A $4 million guarantee from RWJF will increase Inclusiv’s capacity to provide capital to community development credit unions that serve communities of color.

      Increasing Investments in Underserved Communities

      To increase investment to address the social determinants of health in low-income and communities of color, RWJF provided a guarantee of up to $10 million to the Community Investment Guarantee Pool.

      Creating Economic Opportunity in Central Appalachia

      RWJF has made a $5 million investment in the Invest Appalachia Fund (IA Fund) to spur inclusive economic growth and health equity in Central Appalachia

      Increasing Lending to Small-Business Owners of Color

      The 10-year, $18 million guarantee will help development finance agencies in the United States remove some of the lending barriers experienced by people of color when seeking business loans, unlocking more capital to grow their businesses. 

      Improving Community Water Infrastructure

      A $5 million loan from RWJF to Communities Unlimited, a nonprofit community development financial institution, is enabling them to provide loans to water utilities in seven Southern states.

      Improving Quality of Life in New Jersey Through Local Investment

      A $4 million program-related investment from RWJF to the Local Initiatives Support Corporation will seed the creation of the New Jersey CDFI Capital Growth Fund, a program aimed at attracting funding from corporations and financial institutions to invest in small, New Jersey–based CDFIs and similar entities that prioritize hyper-local lending to meet community needs.

      Catalyzing Finance for Racial Equity

      A $5 million loan from RWJF to the Nonprofit Finance Fund supports programs in N.J. to better enable organizations focused on racial equity to acquire, develop, rehabilitate, and operate affordable housing and community facilities in communities that historically have experienced a lack of investment.

      Supporting the Indigenous Finance System on the Mountain Plains

      An RWJF loan will support the Mountain Plains Regional Native Coalition’s efforts to shift the paradigm of community development finance by creating equitable systems of capital and resource allocation and building resilient Native economies.

      Increasing Community Resilience for Climate-Related Events

      Green infrastructure, like parks, street trees, and shrubs on rooftops, improves air quality, reduces flooding and heat islands, improves water quality, and promotes community resilience in the face of extreme weather.

      Resources from Grantees

      Residential Segregation and Health: History, Harms, and Next Steps

      Read more on Health Affairs

      Public Policies to Address Residential Segregation and Improve Health

      Read more on Health Affairs

      Related Content

      Blog Post

      What Does Antiracist Community Development Look Like in Practice?

      An antiracist paradigm for community development along with actions by investors, government, and philanthropy can move the U.S. toward more racially just financing.
      Equitable Community Development Policy and Law Structural Racism
      Blog Post

      Who Finances Healthy Communities?

      When people establish roots, make friendships, raise families, and participate in activities like volunteering or mentoring, a place becomes a community.
      Equitable Community Development
      Library

      Housing Policy and Practice

      Creating safe, affordable, and equitable housing and neighborhood conditions help everyone in a community reach their best possible health and wellbeing.
      Housing
      View all

      Infrastructure

      Promoting health equity through public infrastructure is a critical step toward creating communities and regions where all residents may participate in, and benefit from, decisions that shape the places they call home.
      Infrastructure & Energy

      Impact Investing

      Impact investing expands our toolkit and builds on our decades-long work changing systems and policies to improve health, especially for those facing the greatest obstacles.
      Health Disparities

      Building Community Power

      RWJF supports research and organizing to increase grassroots capacity to shape conditions for healthier communities, especially in low-income communities and communities of color.
      Public and Community Health Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Community Power

      Stay informed

      Sign up for the latest funding alerts, news, research, and perspectives from RWJF.

      Email address already subscribed

      Email address already subscribed. Please check your inbox to manage your subscriptions.

      Subscribed!

      Thank you. You are now subscribed.

      Are you an RWJF Grantee (Past or Present?)
      Are you a European Union Resident?
      Tell us what type of content you want to receive. Select at least one (required).

      Be informed with our twice a month newsletter updating you with relevant news and research around a Culture of Health, as well as the latest funding opportunities.

      Get funded by RWJF: Receive notifications when new funding opportunities are released.

      Receive monthly updates on RWJF-sponsored research that informs many robust health policy debates on Capitol Hill, covering topics like health equity, improving access to quality healthcare, equitable housing, and more.

      Shop talk for researchers. This monthly newsletter covers research news and opportunities from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation.

      Communications expedite action. Get periodic research and expert insights on the best ways to communicate so we can spread what works.

      Sometimes we have news, announcements or opportunities that don't quite fit the subscription parameters above. If you're interested, we'll send you this information under "There's more...". *If you've indicated you are an EU resident, we will only send these communications if you intentionally check this box.
